title: Qdrant Essentials Course (Free)
description: 7-day free course with final project: build a production-ready documentation search engine. Covers points, payloads, distance metrics, HNSW tuning, hybrid search, quantization, multivectors, and the Universal Query API.

title: Vector Databases: from Embeddings to Applications (DeepLearning.AI)
description: Free intermediate course by Sebastian Witalec (Weaviate) covering embeddings, similarity search, ANN, vector DBs overview, sparse/dense/hybrid search, and multilingual search application.

title: Vector Databases Deep Dive (Coursera/Packt)
description: 5-hour intermediate course covering Pinecone, Qdrant, Milvus, and Weaviate with hands-on assignments. Covers core concepts, indexing, querying, and live demos.

title: Vector Database for GenAI (KodeKloud)
description: 4.5-hour course with 50 topics covering vector DB foundations, embedding layers, similarity metrics, building storage on AWS S3, HNSW, quantization, and hands-on labs for semantic search and recommendation engines.

title: Vector Databases and Search (Dataquest)
description: 12-hour free course covering semantic search, ANN algorithms, ChromaDB, chunking strategies, metadata filtering, hybrid search, semantic caching, and a capstone knowledge base search project.

title: Building RAG Systems with Open Models (Coursera)
description: Covers RAG architecture, embedding models, vector DBs (FAISS, ChromaDB, Milvus, Pinecone), indexing strategies, chunking, re-ranking, and production deployment with LangChain.

title: Enterprise RAG: Best Practices and Advanced Techniques (SupportVectors)
description: 12-week live course covering LlamaIndex/LangChain, unstructured ingestion, re-ranking, RAGAS evaluation, GraphRAG, hybrid search, Qdrant vector retrieval, ColBERTv2, and multimodal augmentation.

title: UCLA Extension: Building RAG (COM SCI 910.2)
description: 6-week live online course covering end-to-end RAG architecture, embedding model selection, scalable vector search, RAGAS/TruLens evaluation, cloud deployment, and containerization.

title: Set Up Qdrant Hybrid Search with Sparse + Dense Vectors
description: Step-by-step workflow for configuring hybrid search combining BM25 sparse vectors with dense embeddings.
workflow: Install qdrant-client: pip install qdrant-client[fastembed]
Create collection with sparse vector config
Generate dense embeddings with FastEmbed: model = SparseTextEmbedding(model_name=Qdrant/bm25)
Generate sparse vectors with BM25
Upsert points with both dense and sparse vectors
Search with fusion: query_points with dense and sparse vectors
Tune fusion weights (rrf_k parameter) for optimal recall/precision
Monitor search quality metrics and adjust as needed

title: Production Vector DB Deployment Checklist
description: Step-by-step checklist for deploying a vector database in production with monitoring, backup, and scaling.
workflow: Choose vector DB: Qdrant (GPU-accelerated), Milvus (distributed), Pinecone (managed), Weaviate (hybrid)
Define schema: vector size, distance metric (Cosine/Euclidean/Dot), payload fields
Set up indexing: HNSW parameters (M, ef_construct) based on recall/latency trade-off
Configure quantization: scalar (4x compression) or product (8x) for memory reduction
Set up healthcheck endpoint and monitoring (Qdrant /healthz, Prometheus metrics)
Configure persistence: WAL, snapshots, regular backups to object storage
Set up horizontal scaling: sharding for Milvus, distributed mode for Qdrant
Implement rate limiting and connection pooling
Load test with production data volumes and query patterns
Set up alerting: latency P99, error rate, storage capacity

title: Embedding Model Selection Process
description: Systematic process for selecting the best embedding model for your use case.
workflow: Define requirements: dimensionality, language support, domain (general/code/multilingual)
Check MTEB leaderboard for top models meeting requirements
Download candidate models and test on your domain-specific evaluation set
Benchmark latency: measure embedding time per document for each model
Benchmark quality: retrieval recall@k on your held-out test set
Check model size: ensure it fits in GPU/CPU memory for your serving setup
Consider multilingual needs: E5, BGE-M3, or multilingual-e5 for non-English
Evaluate fine-tuning potential: can you fine-tune on domain data?
Make selection: balance quality, latency, size, and cost
Set up A/B test in production to validate offline benchmarks

title: Data Ingestion Pipeline for Vector DB
description: Step-by-step pipeline for ingesting documents into a vector database with chunking and metadata.
workflow: Extract text from source documents (PDF, HTML, DOCX, etc.)
Clean and normalize text: remove boilerplate, normalize whitespace, deduplicate
Chunk documents: recursive character splitting with 500-1000 token chunks, 10% overlap
Generate embeddings: batch embed with FastEmbed or sentence-transformers
Enrich with metadata: source, page number, section, timestamp, document type
Upsert to vector DB: batch insert with payload and dense/sparse vectors
Set up incremental ingestion: watch for new/modified documents
Implement deletion: soft delete with metadata flags, hard delete with point IDs
Monitor: track ingestion rate, embedding quality, storage growth
Optimize: adjust chunk size based on retrieval quality metrics

title: Qdrant Quantization for Memory Reduction
description: Configure scalar or product quantization in Qdrant to reduce memory usage by 4-8x while maintaining search quality.
workflow: Estimate memory needs: num_points x dim x 4 bytes for float32 vectors
Choose quantization type: scalar (4x reduction) or product (8x)
Enable quantization on collection with update_collection
Re-index collection after enabling quantization
Benchmark search quality: compare recall@k with and without quantization
Monitor memory usage: compare before and after in /metrics endpoint
Adjust quantile parameter: lower quantile = more compression, slightly less accuracy
Set up rescoring: search with quantized vectors, then rescore top-k with full vectors
Validate: run production queries against both and measure quality delta

title: Qdrant Hybrid Search with FastEmbed
description: Python code for setting up Qdrant hybrid search combining dense embeddings with BM25 sparse vectors.

title: ChromaDB Quick Start for RAG
description: Python code for setting up ChromaDB as a local vector store for RAG applications.
code:
import chromadb
from chromadb.utils import embedding_functions

client = chromadb.PersistentClient(path="./chroma_db")
embed_fn = embedding_functions.SentenceTransformerEmbeddingFunction(
    model_name="all-MiniLM-L6-v2"
)
collection = client.get_or_create_collection(
    name="documents",
    embedding_function=embed_fn,
    metadata={"hnsw:space": "cosine"}
)

collection.add(
    documents=["RAG combines retrieval with generation", "Vector search enables semantic matching"],
    ids=["doc1", "doc2"],
    metadatas=[{"source": "blog", "topic": "rag"}, {"source": "docs", "topic": "vectors"}]
)

results = collection.query(
    query_texts=["How does RAG work?"],
    n_results=2,
    where={"topic": "rag"}
)

title: Pinecone Serverless Setup
description: Python code for setting up Pinecone Serverless index with upsert and query.
code:
from pinecone import Pinecone, ServerlessSpec

pc = Pinecone(api_key="your-api-key")

index_name = "documents"
if index_name not in pc.list_indexes().names():
    pc.create_index(
        name=index_name,
        dimension=384,
        metric="cosine",
        spec=ServerlessSpec(cloud="aws", region="us-east-1")
    )

index = pc.Index(index_name)

vectors = [
    {"id": "doc1", "values": [0.1] * 384, "metadata": {"text": "RAG combines retrieval with generation"}},
    {"id": "doc2", "values": [0.2] * 384, "metadata": {"text": "Vector databases enable semantic search"}}
]
index.upsert(vectors=vectors)

results = index.query(
    vector=[0.15] * 384,
    top_k=5,
    include_metadata=True
)

title: Weaviate Hybrid Search Setup
description: Python code for setting up Weaviate with hybrid (sparse + dense) search.
code:
import weaviate
from weaviate.classes.config import Configure

client = weaviate.connect_to_local()

collection = client.collections.create(
    name="Documents",
    vectorizer_config=Configure.Vectorizer.text2vec_transformers(),
    vector_index_config=Configure.VectorIndex.hnsw(
        distance_metric=Configure.VectorIndex.hnsw.distancemetric.COSINE
    )
)

collection.data.insert({"text": "RAG combines retrieval with generation"})
collection.data.insert({"text": "Vector databases enable semantic search"})

results = collection.query.hybrid(
    query="How does RAG work?",
    alpha=0.5,
    limit=5
)
for obj in results.objects:
    print(obj.properties["text"])

title: Milvus Vector Search Pipeline
description: Python code for setting up Milvus with PyMilvus for production vector search.
code:
from pymilvus import MilvusClient, DataType

client = MilvusClient("http://127.0.0.1:19530")

schema = client.create_schema(auto_id=True, enable_dynamic_field=True)
schema.add_field("id", DataType.INT64, is_primary=True)
schema.add_field("vector", DataType.FLOAT_VECTOR, dim=384)
schema.add_field("text", DataType.VARCHAR, max_length=65535)
schema.add_field("source", DataType.VARCHAR, max_length=256)

index_params = client.prepare_index_params()
index_params.add_index(
    field_name="vector",
    index_type="HNSW",
    metric_type="COSINE",
    params={"M": 16, "efConstruction": 256}
)

client.create_collection(
    collection_name="documents",
    schema=schema,
    index_params=index_params
)

title: Qdrant
description: High-performance vector similarity search engine written in Rust. Supports filtering, payload metadata, hybrid search (sparse+dense), quantization, and GPU acceleration. Free with enterprise features.

title: Pinecone
description: Fully managed vector database with serverless and pod-based options. Automatic scaling, metadata filtering, and sparse-dense hybrid search. Best for teams that want zero infrastructure management.

title: Weaviate
description: AI-native vector database with built-in vectorization (modules), GraphQL and REST APIs, hybrid search, multi-tenancy, and automated schema inference. Excellent for rapid prototyping.

title: Milvus
description: Distributed vector database built for scalable similarity search. Supports billion-scale vectors, cloud-native architecture, multi-tenancy, and GPU acceleration. Best for large-scale production deployments.

title: ChromaDB
description: Lightweight, open-source embedding database designed for AI applications. Simple Python API, automatic embedding, and local-first architecture. Best for prototyping and small-scale RAG applications.

title: Hybrid Search Pattern (Sparse + Dense)
description: Combine BM25/sparse vectors for keyword matching with dense vectors for semantic similarity. Fusion methods (RRF, DBSF) merge results for better recall than either alone.

title: Quantization for Memory Reduction
description: Reduce vector DB memory by 4-8x using scalar quantization (INT8) or product quantization (PQ). Trade ~1-3% recall for massive memory savings. Rescoring with full vectors recovers quality.

title: Chunking Strategy Selection Pattern
description: Choose chunking based on content type: recursive character (general), semantic (narrative), sentence (FAQ), document (legal), or sliding window (overlapping). Chunk size 256-1024 tokens with 10% overlap is a good default.

title: Re-ranking Pattern for Improved Retrieval
description: Retrieve top-K (50-100) candidates with vector search, then re-rank with a cross-encoder model for much better precision. Trade latency for quality. Cohere Rerank, BGE-Reranker, and ColBERT are popular choices.

title: Multi-Vector Document Pattern
description: Store multiple vector representations per document: dense (semantic), sparse (BM25), and optionally ColBERT (token-level). Enables hybrid search, late interaction, and better recall at the cost of storage.

title: Qdrant Python Client Quickstart
description: Official Qdrant Python client tutorial covering connection, collection creation, point insertion, filtering, and search with dense and sparse vectors.

title: Weaviate Getting Started Guide
description: Step-by-step guide to setting up Weaviate, creating collections, importing data, and performing hybrid search queries.

title: Milvus Quickstart with PyMilvus
description: Official quickstart for Milvus covering connection, collection creation, index building, data insertion, and vector search.

title: Pinecone Python SDK Guide
description: Official Pinecone Python SDK guide covering index creation, upsert, query with metadata filtering, and namespace management.

title: MTEB Leaderboard for Embedding Model Selection
description: Massive Text Embedding Benchmark leaderboard ranking embedding models across 7 tasks. Essential for model selection.

title: Vector Databases for AI Applications (O'Reilly)
description: Practical guide covering vector search fundamentals, embedding models, HNSW/IVF indexes, production deployment, and real-world RAG applications with multiple vector DB backends.

title: AI-Powered Search (Trey Grainger)
description: Comprehensive guide to building intelligent search systems with AI: semantic search, RAG, learning-to-rank, knowledge graphs, and vector databases. Covers Solr, Elasticsearch, and dedicated vector DBs.

title: Qdrant Vector Search Masterclass
description: Deep dive into Qdrant covering sparse vectors, multivectors, named vectors, payload filtering, quantization, and production deployment patterns.

title: Pinecone Learning Center
description: Official Pinecone learning center with tutorials on indexing strategies, metadata filtering, namespaces, and serverless deployment patterns.

title: Weaviate Academy
description: Free courses covering vector search fundamentals, hybrid search, RAG with Weaviate, multi-tenancy, and production deployment.

title: Build a RAG Pipeline with Qdrant and FastEmbed
description: End-to-end workflow for building a production RAG pipeline with Qdrant, FastEmbed for embeddings, and hybrid search.
workflow: Install dependencies: pip install qdrant-client fastembed
Create Qdrant collection with dense and sparse vector configs
Load documents from source (PDF, web, database)
Chunk documents: recursive character splitting, 500 tokens, 10% overlap
Generate embeddings: fastembed TextEmbedding + SparseTextEmbedding
Upsert to Qdrant with both dense and sparse vectors
Implement hybrid search: dense + sparse fusion
Add re-ranking with cross-encoder for top-k results
Build API with FastAPI for the RAG endpoint
Add conversation memory for multi-turn RAG

title: Migrate from ChromaDB to Qdrant
description: Step-by-step migration from ChromaDB (local dev) to Qdrant (production).
workflow: Export ChromaDB data: collection.get(include=['embeddings','metadatas','documents'])
Install qdrant-client: pip install qdrant-client[fastembed]
Create Qdrant collection matching ChromaDB vector size and distance
Transform ChromaDB data to Qdrant PointStruct format
Upsert all points to Qdrant in batches of 100
Update application code: replace ChromaDB client with Qdrant client
Update search queries: replace collection.query with client.search
Add hybrid search support (upgrade from ChromaDB keyword search)
Run parallel reads from both DBs during migration
Switch traffic to Qdrant and remove ChromaDB dependency

title: Set Up Qdrant with Docker Compose for Production
description: Production Qdrant setup with healthchecks, persistence, and monitoring.
workflow: Create docker-compose.yml with Qdrant service
Configure volumes for persistence: ./qdrant:/qdrant/storage
Set resource limits and GPU reservations
Add healthcheck: python3 urllib http://127.0.0.1:6333/healthz
Configure production.yaml: set snapshot interval, WAL threshold
Set up Prometheus metrics endpoint on /metrics
Add Grafana dashboard for Qdrant metrics
Configure backup: periodic snapshots to S3 or local disk
Set up TLS termination with reverse proxy (nginx/caddy)
Test failover: stop container, verify data persists on restart

title: FAISS Index for Local Vector Search
description: Python code for building and querying a FAISS index for fast local similarity search.
code:
import faiss
import numpy as np
from sentence_transformers import SentenceTransformer

model = SentenceTransformer('all-MiniLM-L6-v2')

documents = [
    'RAG combines retrieval with generation',
    'Vector databases enable semantic search',
    'LoRA reduces fine-tuning memory by 90%',
    'MCP connects AI to external tools and data'
]

# Build FAISS index
embeddings = model.encode(documents)
dimension = embeddings.shape[1]
index = faiss.IndexFlatIP(dimension)
faiss.normalize_L2(embeddings)
index.add(embeddings.astype('float32'))

# Search
query = model.encode(['How does RAG work?'])
faiss.normalize_L2(query)
scores, indices = index.search(query.astype('float32'), k=2)
for i, idx in enumerate(indices[0]):
    print(f'{scores[0][i]:.3f}: {documents[idx]}')

title: FAISS (Facebook AI Similarity Search)
description: High-performance library for efficient similarity search and clustering of dense vectors. Supports GPU acceleration, multiple index types (Flat, IVF, HNSW), and billion-scale search.

title: FastEmbed
description: Lightweight, fast Python library for generating embeddings using quantized ONNX models. 3-5x faster than sentence-transformers with minimal quality loss. Native Qdrant integration.

title: Milvus Attu (GUI)
description: Web-based GUI for managing Milvus vector database. Browse collections, inspect vectors, run queries, monitor performance, and manage indexes visually.

title: HNSW Parameter Tuning Pattern
description: Configure HNSW index parameters for optimal recall/latency tradeoff. M=16-64 controls graph connectivity (higher = better recall, more memory). ef_construct=100-256 controls build quality. ef_search controls query-time accuracy.

title: Namespace and Multi-Tenancy Pattern
description: Isolate tenant data using namespaces (Pinecone), collections (Qdrant/Milvus), or payload filters with tenant IDs. Enables SaaS applications where each tenant has isolated vector data.

title: Vector Database Sharding Pattern
description: Distribute vectors across multiple nodes for horizontal scaling. Qdrant uses shard key-based routing, Milvus uses auto-sharding by primary key. Choose shard key to co-locate related data for efficient filtering.

title: FastEmbed Quickstart Guide
description: Official quickstart for FastEmbed: generate embeddings for text and images, integrate with Qdrant, and configure quantized models for 3-5x speedup.

title: Milvus Partitioning and Sharding Guide
description: Official guide to Milvus partitioning strategies, shard configuration, and collection design for multi-tenant and large-scale deployments.

title: Vector Database in RAG: How It Works, Which One to Pick (2026 Guide)
description: Decision framework guide with HNSW algorithm internals, similarity metrics comparison, detailed breakdown of Pinecone/Qdrant/Weaviate/Milvus/pgvector/Chroma, real cost numbers at 10M and 100M vector scale, and code examples for Qdrant and Weaviate.

title: Milvus Tutorial: Vector DB RAG in 13 Steps [2026]
description: Complete 13-step tutorial with working code: Docker Compose Milvus setup, schema design, Sentence Transformers embeddings, chunking/ingestion, vector search, metadata filtering, HNSW vs IVF_FLAT vs DISKANN index selection, LangChain RAG integration, BM25 hybrid search (Milvus 2.6), benchmarking, Prometheus monitoring, and production auth/TLS.

title: Pinecone vs Qdrant: Which Vector Database Fits Production in 30 Minutes (2026)
description: Benchmarked comparison with full code samples. Pinecone Serverless: 1,000 QPS, 8.7ms p50, $324/mo. Qdrant v1.11.2 (T4 GPU): 4,200 QPS, 12ms p50, ~$378/mo EC2. Covers hybrid search differences, GPU acceleration, and production checklist.
